description: Polish the local-first TypeScript CLI, reports, docs, static product page, community files, CI, examples, and release readiness into a product-quality open-source repository without publishing, deleting user data, or using secrets.

userStories.title: Create baseline audit and backlog
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a current baseline of repository quality, risks, and reusable assets so that later polish work starts from verified facts.
acceptanceCriteria: Review AGENTS.md, README.md, package.json, .github workflows/templates, docs, assets, src, tests, examples, and scripts/ralph files before changing application code
Append scripts/ralph/progress.txt with the baseline inventory, current git status, local-only safety boundaries, and a P0/P1/P2 backlog
Record that CLAUDE.md is absent or document its actual content if it is later added
No secrets, tokens, cookies, browser profiles, private paths, or raw private logs are added to repository files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: current repo already provides npm scripts, Ralph scaffolding, docs, assets, tests, and CI; start by auditing and reusing these instead of adding a new task runner or planning format. Local notes search found no existing project-specific Obsidian note for agent-reliability-kit.

userStories.title: Polish CLI behavior
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want scan, doctor, init, help, version, output formats, paths, and failures to behave predictably so that the tool is safe and clear in local and CI runs.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI help documents every supported command, option, alias, default output location, and local-only safety behavior
Unknown options, invalid --min-score values, invalid --format values, missing option values, and missing target paths return concise actionable errors with exit code 2
scan and doctor do not write outside the requested repository unless --out explicitly points there and the behavior is documented
init never overwrites existing files unless --force is passed, and tests cover both created and skipped files
CLI output remains concise and includes severity, file path, reason, and next action for findings
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: keep the existing lightweight src/cli.ts parser unless the audit proves it cannot meet the criteria; avoid adding a CLI framework dependency for this small command surface.

userStories.title: Strengthen scanners and fixtures
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want scanner coverage for agent instructions, commands, README quality, secrets, GitHub Actions, AI tooling risk, and release readiness so that findings are trustworthy.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover at least one clean repository, one conflicting-agent-instructions repository, one missing-command repository, one unsafe GitHub Actions repository, and one secret-risk repository
Secret-like fixture values are obviously synthetic and all report outputs redact token-like evidence
GitHub Actions scanner checks least-privilege permissions, risky triggers, pipe-to-shell patterns, and validation commands without requiring repository secrets
README and release-readiness checks verify install path, quick start, license, contribution path, package metadata, and expected report outputs
Each finding includes severity, file path when available, reason, and next action
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: extend existing scanners and fixtures in src/scanners and tests/fixtures rather than introducing an external static-analysis engine. Online prior art checked: GitHub Actions permissions docs support least-privilege checks; OpenSSF Scorecard is a mature option, but this project should first dogfood its own local scanner and only add Scorecard as optional documentation or CI guidance if needed.

userStories.title: Polish generated reports
userStories.description: As a maintainer or contributor, I want Markdown, JSON, HTML, SARIF, annotations, and text reports to be readable, redacted, and consistent so that scanner output is useful in local reviews and CI logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Markdown, text, and annotations include severity, file path, reason, and next action for every finding
HTML report is responsive at desktop and mobile widths and does not overflow, overlap, or expose unescaped user-controlled content
SARIF output parses as JSON and includes stable rule IDs and locations where available
Generated reports for secret-risk fixtures contain [redacted] and do not contain raw synthetic token values
Verify in browser

If browser or Playwright validation hits EPERM, WinError 5, restricted token, AppData daemon, or child-process launch failures, switch once to the local no-sandbox/full-access path and do not retry the same sandboxed command repeatedly
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: keep static HTML generation in src/report/html.ts and validate the emitted file in a browser. Online prior art checked: Playwright supports full-page screenshots for static visual validation, but adding Playwright as a dependency is optional and should be justified by actual validation needs.

userStories.title: Polish README, docs, examples, and product page
userStories.description: As a new open-source user, I want the README, docs, examples, assets, and static product page to explain the value, commands, safety model, and report formats clearly so that I can try the tool locally.
acceptanceCriteria: README quick start works from a clean checkout using npm install, npm run build, and node dist/cli.js scan .
README and docs state that scans are local-first and must not include real secrets, private logs, cookies, browser profiles, or private URLs
docs/index.html is a static product page with working local links to README, docs, examples, license, and report concepts
docs/examples/problem-report.md shows a redacted, reproducible issue template without real private data
All documented commands are either verified locally or clearly marked as post-publication examples without running publish
Verify in browser

If browser validation hits EPERM, WinError 5, restricted token, AppData daemon, or child-process launch failures, switch once to the local no-sandbox/full-access path and do not retry the same sandboxed command repeatedly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: npm docs recommend README content for package users, and the repo already has README.md, docs/index.html, docs/examples, and SVG assets. Improve those assets in place instead of replacing the site with a framework.

userStories.title: Harden community files and CI
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want community files, CI, package metadata, and release checks to be safe and ready so that contributors can validate changes without publishing or using secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: .github workflow permissions remain least-privilege and no workflow publishes packages, creates releases, pushes branches, or requires secrets
CI runs npm ci, npm run check, and npm run smoke on pull requests and main pushes
Issue templates, pull request template, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, .gitignore, and package metadata are reviewed for consistency with local-only safety rules
npm pack --dry-run or npm pack --json --dry-run is run locally and progress.txt records the intended package contents without publishing

If credentials are ever required for a later optional integration, implementation must reuse existing logged-in browser sessions, OpenCLI, tool-native credential stores, Cockpit Tools, or the local encrypted AI secret vault instead of asking repeatedly or writing credentials to files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: current CI already uses npm ci, setup-node, npm run check, npm run smoke, and contents: read. Online prior art checked: GitHub Actions docs support explicit GITHUB_TOKEN permissions; npm docs support package files/bin/README review and dry-run packlist checks. Avoid adding publish or release jobs.

userStories.title: Run local verification and self scan
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the repository to verify itself locally so that product-quality claims are backed by repeatable commands.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run check passes
npm run smoke passes after build artifacts are current
A self scan is run with node dist/cli.js scan . --out .tmp/self-scan --format markdown,json,html,sarif,annotations --min-score 0
.tmp/self-scan/report.json parses as JSON and .tmp/self-scan/report.html opens as static HTML
Self-scan findings are triaged into fixed P0/P1 items and documented remaining P2 items in scripts/ralph/progress.txt
No generated .tmp reports are committed unless a story explicitly converts a sanitized sample into a fixture or doc example
Verify in browser

If browser validation hits EPERM, WinError 5, restricted token, AppData daemon, or child-process launch failures, switch once to the local no-sandbox/full-access path and do not retry the same sandboxed command repeatedly
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: dogfood the built CLI and existing smoke script before considering new validation tooling. The Build/Sale/Test/Fix flywheel is captured here by building artifacts, validating product-facing outputs, and turning self-scan failures into a prioritized backlog.

userStories.title: Final review and local commit
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a final review of all accumulated changes and verification evidence so that only safe local repository changes are committed.
acceptanceCriteria: Inspect the accumulated diff or changed files and confirm every story acceptance criterion is either satisfied or documented as remaining risk
Fix all P0 and P1 issues discovered during final review before marking the story complete
Run npm run check, npm run smoke, and the self scan again after final fixes
scripts/ralph/progress.txt contains final verification commands, outcomes, remaining P2 risks, and the local commit hash
git status shows only expected local repository changes before commit and a clean or intentionally documented state after commit
Create only a local commit for the target repo changes; do not push branches, publish to npm, create GitHub releases, delete user data, or use secrets
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse decision: use standard git local commit workflow and existing npm verification commands. Keep release readiness as documentation and dry-run evidence only; publishing and remote operations remain out of scope unless the user explicitly asks later.
